message:
feat(merge): auto-resolve all field conflicts deterministically (bd-6l8)

Implement deterministic auto-resolve rules for all merge conflicts:
- Title/Description: side with latest updated_at wins
- Notes: concatenate both sides with separator
- Priority: higher priority wins (lower number)
- IssueType: local (left) wins
- Status: closed wins (existing)

Also fixed bug in isTimeAfter where invalid t1 incorrectly beat valid t2.

Removed unused conflict generation code (hasConflict, makeConflict,
makeConflictWithBase, issuesEqual, cmp import).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-5bj","title":"Registry has cross-process race condition","description":"The global daemon registry (~/.beads/registry.json) can be corrupted when multiple daemons from different workspaces write simultaneously.

**Root cause:**
- Registry uses an in-process mutex but no file-level locking
- Register() and Unregister() release the mutex between read and write
- Multiple daemon processes can interleave their read-modify-write cycles

**Evidence:**
Found registry.json with double closing bracket: `]]` instead of `]`

**Fix options:**
1. Use file locking (flock/fcntl) around the entire read-modify-write cycle
2. Use atomic write pattern (write to temp file, rename)
3. Both (belt and suspenders)

**Files:**
- internal/daemon/registry.go:46-64 (readEntries)
- internal/daemon/registry.go:67-87 (writeEntries)
- internal/daemon/registry.go:90-108 (Register - the race window)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-27T13:55:50.426188-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T14:07:06.22622-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-27T14:07:06.22622-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-63l","title":"bd hooks install fails in git worktrees","description":"When bd is used in a git worktree, bd hooks install fails with 'mkdir .git: not a directory' because .git is a file (gitdir pointer) not a directory. Beads should detect and follow the .git gitdir pointer to install hooks in the correct location. This blocks normal worktree workflows.

## Symptoms of this bug:
- Git hooks don't install automatically
- Auto-sync doesn't run (5-second debounce)
- Hash mismatch warnings in bd output
- Daemon fails to start with 'auto_start_failed'

## Workaround:
Use `git rev-parse --git-dir` to find the actual hooks directory and copy hooks manually:
```bash
mkdir -p $(git rev-parse --git-dir)/hooks
cp -r .beads-hooks/* $(git rev-parse --git-dir)/hooks/
```","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-29T00:27:59.111163003-07:00","updated_at":"2025-11-29T23:20:04.196608-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-29T23:20:01.394894-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-6ic","title":"Second worktree test","description":"","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T00:40:28.768125-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-30T00:40:58.06615-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-30T00:40:58.06615-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-6l8","title":"Auto-resolve all field conflicts in merge.go","description":"Eliminate manual conflict resolution by adding deterministic auto-resolve rules for all fields:

- Priority: higher priority wins (lower number = more urgent)
- IssueType: local wins
- Notes: concatenate both sides with separator
- Title: side with latest updated_at on the issue wins
- Description: side with latest updated_at on the issue wins

Currently true conflicts (both sides changed same field to different values) fail sync. With this change, NO conflicts ever require manual resolution.

Location: internal/merge/merge.go

 {"id":"bd-736d","title":"Refactor path canonicalization into helper function","description":"The path canonicalization logic (filepath.Abs + EvalSymlinks) is duplicated in 3 places:

- cmd/bd/main.go:446-451 (--no-db cleanup)

Refactor to use a single implementation with a skipGit bool parameter or extract shared logic into helper functions to reduce ~200 lines of duplication.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-01T17:38:02.632264-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-01T21:08:11.952459-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-01T21:08:11.952459-08:00","close_reason":"Refactored createSyncFunc and createLocalSyncFunc to use shared performSync implementation. Reduced 54 lines of duplication."}
 {"id":"bd-7ch","title":"Auto-push after merge with safety check","description":"Make bd sync a true one-command solution by auto-pushing after successful content merge.

Behavior:
- After successful content merge, auto-push by default
- Safety check: detect when >50% issues vanished AND >5 existed before
- On safety check trigger: warn but still push (do not block happy path)

Vanished means issues removed from issues.jsonl entirely, NOT status=closed (closed is legitimate swarm completion).

Location: internal/syncbranch/worktree.go

This enables dynamic tips (not just pre-defined ones) to be shown with custom priority and frequency.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-11T23:29:46.645583-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T17:52:35.096882-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T17:52:35.096882-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-81a","depends_on_id":"bd-d4i","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-11T23:29:46.646327-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-8a5","title":"Refactor: deduplicate FindJSONLInDir and FindJSONLPath","description":"## Background

After fixing bd-tqo, we now have two nearly identical functions for finding the JSONL file:
- `autoimport.FindJSONLInDir(dbDir string)` in internal/autoimport/autoimport.go
- `beads.FindJSONLPath(dbPath string)` in internal/beads/beads.go

Both implement the same logic:
1. Prefer issues.jsonl
2. Fall back to beads.jsonl for legacy support
3. Skip deletions.jsonl and merge artifacts
4. Default to issues.jsonl if nothing found

## Problem

Code duplication means bug fixes need to be applied in multiple places (as we just experienced with bd-tqo).

## Proposed Solution

Extract shared logic to a utility package that both can import. Options:
1. Create `internal/jsonlpath` package with the core logic
2. Have `autoimport` import `beads` and call `FindJSONLPath` (but APIs differ slightly)
3. Move to `internal/utils` if appropriate

Need to verify no import cycles would be created.

## Affected Files
- internal/autoimport/autoimport.go
- internal/beads/beads.go","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-26T23:45:18.974339-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-29T22:06:06.330185-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T23:07:08.912247-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-8an","title":"bd import auto-detects wrong prefix from directory name instead of issue IDs","description":"When importing issues.jsonl into a fresh database, 'bd import' prints:

  ✓ Initialized database with prefix 'beads' (detected from issues)

But the issues all have prefix 'bd-' (e.g., bd-03r). It appears to be detecting the prefix from the directory name (.beads/) rather than from the actual issue IDs in the JSONL.

This causes import to fail with:
  validate ID prefix for bd-03r: issue ID 'bd-03r' does not match configured prefix 'beads'

Workaround: Run 'bd config set issue_prefix bd' before import, or use 'bd init --prefix bd'.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-26T22:28:01.582564-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T22:17:12.607316-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-27T22:38:48.971617-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-8ib","title":"Update git hooks to be sync.branch aware","description":"## Problem

The pre-push hook blocks pushes when .beads/issues.jsonl has uncommitted changes. But with sync.branch configured, those changes are intentionally NOT committed to main - they go to the sync branch via worktree.

## Current Behavior

1. User configures sync.branch=beads-sync
2. bd sync commits changes to beads-sync via worktree  
3. Local .beads/issues.jsonl is updated (needed for import)
4. git push to main triggers pre-push hook
5. Hook sees uncommitted .beads changes and blocks push
6. User must use --no-verify to push

## Expected Behavior

When sync.branch is configured, the pre-push hook should:
1. Check if sync.branch is set (bd config get sync.branch)
2. If set, skip the .beads uncommitted check OR
3. Verify changes are committed to the sync branch instead

## Affected Files

- examples/git-hooks/pre-push
- examples/git-hooks/pre-commit (may also need update)

## Workaround

Use git push --no-verify","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T00:43:40.991951-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-30T11:12:09.91242-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-30T11:12:09.91242-08:00"}

For consistency with IssueType (where local/left wins), equal timestamps should prefer left. Minor inconsistency.","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-02T20:14:59.898345-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-02T20:14:59.898345-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-93d","title":"Jira export script (jsonl2jira.py)","description":"Create a Python script to push beads issues to Jira.

**Requires**: Jira import script to be complete first (need external_ref matching logic working)

**Features needed**:
- Create new Jira issues from beads issues without external_ref
- Update existing Jira issues matched by external_ref
- Map beads fields back to Jira fields
- Handle Jira workflow transitions (status changes may need transitions)
- Support custom field mapping for design/acceptance_criteria/notes

**Challenges**:
- Jira status changes often require workflow transitions, not direct updates
- Need to discover valid transitions via API
- Custom fields vary by Jira instance

**Usage**:
```bash
bd export | python jsonl2jira.py --create-only  # Only create, don't update
bd export | python jsonl2jira.py                 # Create and update
```

**After creation**: Sets external_ref on beads issue to link back","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-30T12:56:14.266357-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-30T15:19:40.264737-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-30T15:19:40.264737-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-93d","depends_on_id":"bd-qvj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-11-30T12:56:44.652391-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-93d","depends_on_id":"bd-tjn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-30T12:56:54.941116-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}

 {"id":"bd-c8x","title":"Don't search parent directories for .beads databases","description":"bd currently walks up the directory tree looking for .beads directories, which can find unrelated databases (e.g., ~/.beads). This causes confusing warnings and potential data pollution.

Should either:
1. Stop at git root (don't search above it)
2. Only use explicit BEADS_DB env var or local .beads
3. At minimum, don't search in home directory","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-27T22:10:41.992686-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-28T22:17:12.607956-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-28T22:15:55.878353-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-clg","title":"bd jira sync command","description":"Add a built-in bd command for Jira synchronization.

**Requires**: Both import and export scripts working

**Features**:
- `bd jira sync --pull` - Import from Jira to beads
- `bd jira sync --push` - Export from beads to Jira
- `bd jira sync` - Bidirectional (pull then push, with conflict resolution)
- `bd jira status` - Show sync status and last sync time

**Conflict resolution**:
- Timestamp-based: newer update wins
- Option for --prefer-local or --prefer-jira to override
- Interactive mode for manual conflict resolution (optional)

**Integration**:
- Uses jira.* config settings from bd config
- Stores last sync timestamp in config
- Logs sync activity for audit

**Stretch goals**:
- Webhook integration for real-time sync
- Selective sync by JQL filter","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-30T12:56:27.716537-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-30T15:25:37.896045-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-30T15:25:37.896045-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-clg","depends_on_id":"bd-qvj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-11-30T12:56:49.796568-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-clg","depends_on_id":"bd-tjn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-30T12:57:00.075288-08:00","created_by":"stevey"},{"issue_id":"bd-clg","depends_on_id":"bd-93d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-11-30T12:57:05.206431-08:00","created_by":"stevey"}]}
 {"id":"bd-co0","title":"Imperator does not know its own mail identity","description":"","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-30T22:53:49.963803-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-01T22:01:26.953199-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-01T22:01:26.953199-08:00"}
+{"id":"bd-d0t","title":"Priority 0 in merge may incorrectly win over set priorities","description":"In mergePriority(), priority 0 (which may mean 'unset' due to Go's zero value) beats any explicitly set priority like P1, P2, etc. Should probably treat 0 as 'no priority set' and not let it win conflicts.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-02T20:14:58.906543-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-02T20:14:58.906543-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-d4i","title":"Create tip system infrastructure for contextual hints","description":"Implement a tip/hint system that shows helpful contextual messages after successful commands. This is different from the existing error-path "Hint:" messages - tips appear on success paths to educate users about features they might not know about.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-11T23:29:15.693956-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-25T17:47:30.747566-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-25T17:47:30.747566-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-dmb","title":"Fresh clone: bd should suggest 'bd init' when no database exists","description":"On a fresh clone of a repo using beads, running `bd stats` or `bd list` gives a cryptic error:

```
Error: failed to open database: post-migration validation failed: migration invariants failed:
  - required_config_present: required config key missing: issue_prefix (database has 2 issues)
```

**Expected**: A helpful message like:
```
No database found. This appears to be a fresh clone.
Run 'bd init --prefix <prefix>' to hydrate from the committed JSONL file.
Found: .beads/beads.jsonl (38 issues)
```

**Why this matters**: The current UX is confusing for new contributors or fresh clones. The happy path should be obvious.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-27T20:21:04.947959-08:00","updated_at":"2025-11-27T22:40:11.654051-08:00","closed_at":"2025-11-27T22:40:11.654051-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-e166","title":"Improve timestamp comparison readability in import","description":"The timestamp comparison logic uses double-negative which can be confusing:

Current code:
if !incoming.UpdatedAt.After(existing.UpdatedAt) {
    // skip update
}

More readable:
if incoming.UpdatedAt.After(existing.UpdatedAt) {
    // perform update
} else {
    // skip (local is newer)
}

This is a minor refactor for code clarity.

Related: bd-1022
